page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the average tier across most standard compute and memory workloads. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1866.58 and a multi-core score of 15529.0. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only small block speeds of 845.59 GB/sec and large block write speeds of 58.29 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI, which is reflected in a Passmark CPU encryption score of 14610.14.

In terms of application-level performance, the instance handles static web serving at 21.41 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 15.86 million operations per second. Large language model inference benchmarks indicate strong performance for text generation on small 135M models at 271.33 tokens per second, while medium 7B model text generation operates at an average speed of 11.91 tokens per second. The balanced resource allocation makes this instance qualitatively cost-effective for general-purpose enterprise workloads, software compilation, and database operations that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark results place this server in the average tier for general compute, with a Passmark CPU score of 21105.84 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 15529.0. It demonstrates strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 271.33 tokens per second. Supported by AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration, this instance is suited for balanced workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and database operations.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 64 GB RAM for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
